comment.en: Codex CLI is OpenAI's local coding agent for the terminal. Install it globally with npm, then run it from the root of the project you want Codex to inspect or edit. See the Codex CLI docs for current install and auth options.

inserted.text:
codex version 0.1.2504251714

Usage: codex [options] [prompt]

Options:
  -m, --model <model>    Model to use (default: codex-mini-latest)
  -q, --quiet            Non-interactive, quiet mode
  --full-auto            Skip all confirmations
  -h, --help             Display help

comment.en: Verify that the codex command is on your PATH and inspect the available subcommands and flags.
Follow the browser-based sign-in flow. Codex stores credentials locally so you only need to do this once.

inserted.text:
Welcome to Codex!
Working directory: /path/to/your/project

Type a message to get started.

comment.en: Starting Codex inside a project gives it the local files and instructions it needs for a useful coding session. For this tutorial's demo, Codex was started in a small folder named demo2 and asked to build a Brownian motion webpage.
